title: sorbus aria subsp. meridionalis
intro: Sorbus aria subsp. meridionalis, also known as the Southern Whitebeam, is a subspecies of the Whitebeam plant, which is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to southern Europe and North Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antibacterial properties, and is used to treat various health issues such as digestive problems, respiratory infections, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Sorbus aria subsp. meridionalis include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system, making it a valuable natural remedy for maintaining overall health and well-being. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be consumed as teas, added to food, or taken as supplements to reap its medicinal benefits.
